sections.title: The Beginning of MEDIVARA
content: Indonesia requires more healthcare institutions that are both accessible and deeply trusted. The country needs more than just new hospital buildings; it needs healthcare institutions that are professionally managed, sustainable, and capable of delivering quality care that meets international standards.

However, operating and establishing a hospital is inherently complex. Many hospitals begin with noble intentions—driven by family visions, community needs, or a desire to serve society. But without the essential strategy, systems, and leadership, the process of running a hospital can become extremely difficult.
MEDIVARA was designed to address this challenge, empowering hospitals to become stronger, operate more effectively, and better serve their communities.

sections.title: The Challenges facing Hospitals in Indonesia
sections.intro: Even with increasing demand for healthcare, many hospitals face difficulties in achieving their maximum potential. Common obstacles include:
bulletPoints: Slow pace of return on investment
Low patient occupancy during the initial years
Consequently, despite the continuous growth in healthcare demand, many hospitals are unable to fully realize their potential.

subsections.title: The Gap
subsections.content: A critical gap exists. On one side, communities demand better healthcare services. On the other, many hospitals need the right support to grow professionally and sustainably. MEDIVARA was created to bridge this gap.

subsections.title: Our Philosophy
subsections.intro: MEDIVARA believes that strong hospitals and healthcare institutions are not built merely with capital and physical infrastructure. They are built with:
bulletPoints: A strong commitment to serving the community
subsections.outro: MEDIVARA does not replace the vision of hospital owners - we help strengthen and realize it.

sections.title: Medivara, Your Trusted Business Partner
sections.intro: We are not merely advisors. We work alongside hospital owners, investors, and healthcare institutions to build strong and sustainable systems, from strategy to operations. Our expertise includes:
sections.outro: Our purpose is simple: to help healthcare institutions become stronger, sustainable, and trusted by the communities they serve.

items.title: Major Transformation at Dharma Nugraha Hospital: More Modern, Comfortable, and Patient-Centered Services
items.excerpt: Dharma Nugraha Hospital has officially launched its new face through a comprehensive transformation focusing on patient comfort, facility modernization, and quality of care improvement.
items.content:
ARCOM-MEDIA, Jakarta. The capital's healthcare landscape has received a breath of fresh air. Dharma Nugraha Hospital has officially launched its new face through a comprehensive transformation focusing on patient comfort, facility modernization, and quality of care improvement.

This move is not merely a physical update, but a holistic change encompassing the entire patient experience from start to finish.

Established in 1996, Dharma Nugraha Hospital continuously strives to remain relevant amid the increasingly complex demands of healthcare, without abandoning core values such as empathy, sincerity, and trust.

One of the most immediately noticeable changes is the hospital's interior, which is now warmer and more modern. The previously formal and stiff atmosphere has been transformed into something more welcoming and soothing, providing psychological comfort for both patients and their families.

Beyond aesthetics, improvements have also been made to medical services. Dharma Nugraha Hospital now offers a more comprehensive range of specialist services, including internal medicine, maternal and child health, general surgery and oncology, neurology, cardiology, medical rehabilitation, and ENT. Complete dental health services are also available for the entire family.

Supporting facilities such as a 24-hour emergency unit, laboratory, radiology, and physiotherapy have been strengthened to ensure that diagnosis and treatment processes run quickly and accurately. This forms an important part of improving service quality based on speed and precision.

Addressing the needs of today's dynamic society, the hospital now offers home service and flexible medical check-up programs. Patients can access health services not only at the hospital, but also at home or even in the workplace.

In its transformation process, Dharma Nugraha Hospital partnered with Medivara – Healthcare Consulting as a strategic partner. This collaboration covers improvements to operational systems, hospital information system development, and enhancement of governance and service quality to ensure a more structured and sustainable transformation.

Director PT and Owner of Dharma Nugraha Hospital, Sekar Dewi Dinawati Tjindarbumi, Sp.THT-KL., emphasized that this transformation focuses on the human aspect of healthcare. "This is not just about new facilities, but about how we bring back the essence of more humane service. We want patients to feel safe, comfortable, and confident that they are in the right place," said Sekar Dewi at the Introduction of the New Face of Dharma Nugraha Hospital, Tuesday (21/4/2026), at the lobby of Dharma Nugraha Hospital, Jalan Balai Pustaka Baru No.19, Rawamangun, East Jakarta.

Located in the strategic Rawamangun area of East Jakarta, Dharma Nugraha Hospital has also partnered with various insurance providers, making healthcare services more accessible to the wider community.

With nearly three decades of experience and excellence in oncology treatment, this transformation further strengthens Dharma Nugraha Hospital's position as a hospital ready to compete in the era of modern healthcare—combining technology, professionalism, and a humanistic touch in one integrated service.
items.author: RED

items.title: Access to Quality Healthcare in Focus: Hospitals Called to Be More Humane and Affordable
items.excerpt: Society needs access to quality, fast, and affordable healthcare, particularly in metropolitan areas like Jakarta.
items.content:
Society needs access to quality, fast, and affordable healthcare, particularly in metropolitan areas like Jakarta. With rising healthcare costs, the presence of medical facilities with comprehensive services and structured systems has become critical for equitable health access.

In East Jakarta, Dharma Nugraha Hospital—established in 1996—has undergone a comprehensive transformation covering not only infrastructure but also service systems and patient approach. The hospital provides inpatient and outpatient services with specializations in internal medicine, cardiology, neurology, and obstetrics-gynecology.

Supporting facilities have been strengthened with a 24-hour emergency service, laboratory, radiology, and physiotherapy to accelerate diagnosis and treatment. Innovations also include preventive health screenings and home visit services for patients with limited mobility.

The operational transformation involved MEDIVARA Healthcare Consulting to improve the hospital information system and service standards. This approach reflects the modern healthcare industry trend of integrating digitalization and operational efficiency to reduce costs while improving quality.

However, service improvement is not solely dependent on physical facilities. Director and owner Sekar Dewi Dinawati Tjindarbumi emphasized that "this transformation is not just about facilities, but about how patients feel comfortable, cared for" and confident in receiving the right treatment—the human dimension is the primary focus in modern healthcare delivery.
items.author: Rian Alfianto

items.title: Glimpsing the New Face of Dharma Nugraha Hospital: More Modern and Comfortable
items.excerpt: Dharma Nugraha Hospital has introduced its latest transformation, featuring modern facilities with a more humanistic approach.
items.content:
Dharma Nugraha Hospital has introduced its latest transformation, featuring modern facilities with a more humanistic approach. This healthcare institution, founded in 1996, continues to evolve while maintaining its core values: sincerity, empathy, and trust.

The changes include a more modern and calming interior design, creating a comfortable atmosphere for patients and families. The hospital provides a range of medical specializations: internal medicine, maternal and child health, general surgery, oncology, neurology, cardiology, medical rehabilitation, ENT, and comprehensive dental services.

Supporting facilities include a 24-hour emergency unit, laboratory, radiology, and physiotherapy. Flexible service innovations include home service and medical check-ups that can be performed at the patient's home or workplace.

The hospital partnered with MEDIVARA Healthcare Consulting to strengthen the operational system, develop the hospital information system, and improve governance and service quality in a professional manner.

Director and owner Sekar Dewi Dinawati Tjindarbumi emphasized that this transformation is not merely a facility update, but an effort to restore the essence of more humane service—ensuring every patient feels cared for and confident in receiving the best treatment.

With experienced doctors, professional medical personnel, and partnerships with various insurance providers, the hospital facilitates community access to quality services. Special expertise in oncology treatment has been the foundation of community trust for nearly three decades.
items.author: Ida Umy Rasyidah

items.title: Hospital Industry Grows More Competitive: System Transformation Is the Key to Survival
items.excerpt: Amid the ever-evolving healthcare industry, hospitals in Indonesia face increasingly complex challenges. They are not only required to deliver quality medical services, but also to maintain operational sustainability amid cost pressures, competition, and regulatory changes.
items.content:
Amid the ever-evolving healthcare industry, hospitals in Indonesia face increasingly complex challenges. They are not only required to deliver quality medical services, but also to maintain operational sustainability amid cost pressures, competition, and regulatory changes.

Behind impressive buildings and services that appear to run smoothly, many healthcare institutions face structural challenges that are not always visible on the surface.

Over-reliance on a handful of key doctors, non-standardized operational systems, and ever-increasing funding needs are recurring problems. Many hospitals have grown "naturally" — evolving over time without a truly solid systemic foundation.

As a result, operations continue to run, but they are difficult to measure and control. Performance may appear stable, yet it is vulnerable when pressure arrives. In many cases, organizations rely more on individuals than on robust systems.

From Surviving to Transforming

This is the point at which many healthcare institutions begin to realize that mere survival is not enough. Transformation has become an unavoidable necessity. Yet the greatest challenge lies not in designing a strategy, but in executing it. This is where MEDIVARA Healthcare Consulting becomes relevant — not merely as a consultant, but as an execution partner directly involved in the transformation process, ensuring that change does not stop on paper.

"Strategy is easy to create. The hard part is making sure that strategy actually happens on the ground — and that is where we work," said Rima Fatmasari, Lead Advisor for Clinical Operations & Governance Excellence at MEDIVARA. The approach MEDIVARA brings emphasizes one fundamental principle: transformation will only succeed if supported by strong systems and disciplined execution.

Building a Sustainable System

Building on this principle, MEDIVARA integrates various critical aspects of hospital organization into a single structured framework — spanning governance, clinical operations, and human resource development.

The focus is not merely on fixing what appears to be broken, but on building a foundation capable of maintaining quality consistently over the long term. This is achieved through the development of a clear and accountable organizational structure, the establishment of standardized operational processes that reduce dependence on specific individuals, and simultaneous efforts to optimize both service performance and financial outcomes. At the same time, MEDIVARA drives the formation of a collaborative, patient-centered work culture, so that change occurs not only in systems but also in how teams work within the organization.

"Many hospitals want to change, but they stop at the planning stage. The real challenge is execution. That is where we come in — ensuring that transformation actually takes place and delivers impact," Rima added.

Case Study: Measurable Transformation

This approach is reflected in the transformation of Dharma Nugraha Hospital, an institution with a long history in oncology. Prior to the transformation, the hospital faced challenges in operational consistency and inter-unit coordination. Non-standardized systems made performance difficult to manage comprehensively, while dependence on key individuals was considerable.

The transformation was focused on a comprehensive overhaul of systems. "Transformation is not about fixing what is broken — it is about building a system that makes the organization no longer easily broken" became the guiding principle of the process.

Within less than a year of MEDIVARA's involvement, changes began to show. Operational systems became more structured, management stronger, and service quality improved. The impact extended beyond internal efficiency to patient experience and public perception.

"MEDIVARA did not just provide guidance — they truly walked alongside us throughout this transformation. The changes that occurred were not just on paper; we felt them in our systems, our team, and in patient trust," said Sekar Dewi Dinawati Tjindarbumi, Director and Owner of Dharma Nugraha Hospital.

Addressing the Healthcare Industry Challenge

This transformation demonstrates that the core challenge for many healthcare institutions lies not in a lack of potential, but in the absence of strong systems and consistent execution. Amid increasingly complex industry dynamics — from accreditation requirements and cost efficiency to ever-rising patient expectations — hospitals are required to adapt comprehensively, not partially.

Going forward, the institutions that will survive and thrive will not simply be those with the best facilities, but those with a solid organizational foundation, systems that operate consistently, and the ability to execute strategies with discipline across every level of operations.

Real Transformation

Ultimately, transformation in the healthcare sector is not merely about changing appearances or adding facilities. More than that, it is about building an organization that is resilient under pressure and capable of delivering consistent care. In this context, MEDIVARA's role as an implementation partner becomes increasingly important — not only in charting the direction of change, but in ensuring that change truly happens.

Because amid the complexity of today's healthcare industry, transformation is no longer a choice — it is a necessity. Not merely to survive, but to remain relevant and sustainable.
items.author: Ihfa Firdausya
